headline: Musk: 'We expect fully autonomous vehicles in a quarter to half of the US' by end 2026 — robotaxis targeting 'dozens of major cities'; same promise made for 2025 and missed; aspirational language flagged by analysts

TSLA trading ~$393 (–2%) with 48 hours to Q1 earnings call — 3 key bull catalysts per Techi.com: Cybercab unit target >20K annualized, robotaxi revenue-per-mile disclosure, and gross margin ex-credits >19%
~1 in 5 Cybertruck Q1 2026 registrations were internal SpaceX transfers — organic civilian retail demand materially weaker than headline numbers; bear case on Cybertruck profitability strengthened
14 Cybercab units WITHOUT steering wheels spotted at Giga Texas — production-spec autonomous Cybercabs rolling off line; original no-steering-wheel design confirmed; production ramp accelerating
Optimus robot live at Tesla Boston Boylston Street showroom for Marathon Monday — hundreds of thousands of spectators + international broadcast; zero ad spend PR at massive scale
Electrek: Dallas/Houston robotaxi expansion 'looks like another stock pump before earnings' — small geofences, limited vehicles; skepticism growing on autonomy pace ahead of Apr 22 Q1 call
Tesla launches unsupervised robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston — 573-vehicle Model Y fleet, no driver or monitor in front seats; first multi-city expansion beyond Austin confirmed live
Optimus Gen 3 hand and arm details revealed in new patents — new joint architecture and dexterity upgrades; production design locked per blueprint filings
